name: capture-structured-product
description: Save a product from a real retail site that exposes JSON-LD or Open Graph data
given: a live product page (Uniqlo, Nike, Patagonia, Quince) open in the browser
when: the user clicks Save / the + and the extension scrapes the active tab
then: the saved item has a real title, an http(s) product image, a valid category, and a price when the site exposes one
validates: JSON-LD (Product, ProductGroup+hasVariant, AggregateOffer) -> Open Graph -> DOM price/image fallback pipeline

name: productgroup-with-variants
description: Nike-style ProductGroup JSON-LD with price nested under hasVariant[].offers
given: a Nike product page using @type ProductGroup
when: scrapeProduct runs on the captured HTML
then: title, brand (Nike, not 'Nike.com'), and price ($115) are all extracted
validates: ProductGroup detection + hasVariant offer price extraction + brand-not-from-og:site_name

name: live-page-image-hint
description: Pick the real product hero image using live naturalWidth, skipping logos/SVG badges
given: a product page whose largest DOM image is a logo or B-corp SVG badge
when: the in-page grab computes the best image by rendered area, skipping junk patterns
then: a logo/sprite/svg is never chosen as the product image; protocol-relative URLs are absolutized
validates: pageGrab() image selection + JUNK_IMAGE filter + URL absolutization

name: known-limitation-spa
description: A client-rendered SPA with no structured data (Allbirds) degrades gracefully
given: a product page that ships no JSON-LD and only a marketing og:title + logo og:image
then: it still saves a title/image/category without crashing; price may be missing — documented limitation, not a failure
validates: graceful degradation when no structured data exists
